title: DDPM's Practical Limitations
concept: The original DDPM required around 1000 sequential sampling steps, making generation extremely slow compared to a GAN's single forward pass.

exercises.prompt: When you bake a cake, do you get it instantly or does it take time in the oven?

options.text: It takes time, step by step

exercises.explain: Right! Baking takes many small steps over time. Some AI image generators work the same way — slowly, step by step — instead of all at once.

cards.title: Why Is This AI So Slow?
cards.body: Imagine asking a friend to draw you a picture, but they insist on erasing and redrawing it 1000 times before showing you the final result. That's basically how the original DDPM (Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model) makes images — and it takes forever compared to other AI methods!

cards.title: What's the Limitation?
cards.body: The original DDPM needs about 1000 small steps, one after another, to turn random noise into a clear image. Each step depends on the one before it, so they can't be skipped or rushed. This makes DDPM very slow at generating images compared to other AI models.

cards.title: Climbing Down a Ladder
cards.body: Think of DDPM like climbing down a 1000-rung ladder one rung at a time — you can't jump to the bottom, you must take every single step in order. A GAN, on the other hand, is like jumping straight to the ground in one leap. Both get you down, but one is much faster!

cards.title: Generating One Picture
cards.body: To create a single image of a cat, DDPM starts with pure random noise and slowly removes a tiny bit of noise 1000 times in a row until the cat appears. A GAN would instead generate that same cat image in just one quick pass through its network.

cards.title: Myth: More Steps Means Better AI
cards.body: Myth: Taking 1000 steps must mean DDPM is 'smarter' or always better. Truth: Those 1000 steps are actually a big weakness — they make DDPM much slower to use. Researchers had to invent faster techniques later (like DDIM) specifically to fix this slowness problem.

cards.title: Speed Showdown
cards.body: Here's how the two approaches compare when generating a single image:

left.text: About 1000 slow, sequential steps to remove noise bit by bit

right.text: Just 1 single fast forward pass to create the image

exercises.prompt: What is the main practical limitation of the original DDPM?

options.text: It requires around 1000 sequential steps, making it very slow
It only works on small images

exercises.explain: Correct! DDPM's biggest practical drawback is needing about 1000 sequential steps to generate one image, making it much slower than models like GANs.

exercises.prompt: A GAN generates an image in a single forward pass, while the original DDPM needs about 1000 steps.
exercises.explain: True! This is exactly why DDPM is so much slower than GANs at generating images — it needs many sequential steps instead of one quick pass.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about DDPM's limitation.
exercises.sentence: The original DDPM needed around ___ sequential steps to generate a single image, making it much slower than a GAN.
exercises.explain: The original DDPM required about 1000 sequential denoising steps per image, which made generation very slow.

exercises.prompt: Why can't DDPM's 1000 steps just be done all at once, in parallel, like a GAN's single pass?

options.text: Because each step depends on the result of the previous step, so they must happen in order
Because the computer doesn't have enough memory ever
Because DDPM doesn't use noise at all

exercises.explain: Exactly right! Each denoising step relies on the output of the step before it, so they must be done one after another (sequentially), not all at once.

exercises.prompt: A student says: 'DDPM and GANs both create images equally fast because they're both AI models.' What's wrong with this statement?

options.text: Nothing is wrong — they are equally fast
DDPM is actually much slower because it needs about 1000 sequential steps, while a GAN only needs one forward pass
GANs are slower because they use more noise
DDPM doesn't generate images at all

exercises.explain: Correct! Being 'both AI models' doesn't mean equal speed. DDPM's original design requires around 1000 sequential denoising steps to build one image, while a GAN produces an image in a single quick forward pass — a huge speed difference in practice.
